mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payroll employment raises aggregate wage income and consumer spending, which drives corporate revenue expansion and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following positive month-over-month PAYEMS changes are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5.0 percent reflects restrictive monetary policy that elevates discount rates and borrowing costs, reducing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%, or the Rank IC between FEDFUNDS levels and forward returns is positive over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint increases in the unemployment rate and consumer price index reduce real household income and corporate margins simultaneously, compressing earnings multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint interaction of UNRATE and CPIAUCSL delivers a Rank IC magnitude below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the directional accuracy of the combined signal is below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a month-over-month decline in industrial production signals capacity constraints and impending margin compression, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following INDPRO declines are not significantly negative during late-cycle periods, or the signal Rank IC is positive over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month contraction in real retail sales signals weakening consumer demand that translates into lower corporate revenues and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative RSAFS growth is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in residential construction starts reflects improving construction activity and household formation, leading to broad sectoral spillovers and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following month-over-month increases in HOUST do not significantly exceed zero (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden increase in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over two months signals labor market deterioration that depresses consumption and investment, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following a 0.3 percentage point jump in UNRATE are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above trend raises earnings expectations and reduces recession probability, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth does not exhibit a positive Rank IC with 60-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling window, or positive GDP surprises fail to predict positive returns (t-test p > 0.05).
